calendar.matters:
Office Of The Attorney General Motion To Stay Case Pending Resolution Of Similar Litigation In Federal Court
Off Calendar Oct-29-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Office Of The Attorney General Motion To Stay Case Pending Resolution Of Similar Litigation In Federal Court IS A DUPLICATE ENTRY.

Motion To Stay Case Pending Resolution Of Similar Litigation In Federal Court
Off Calendar Oct-29-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES - ATTY. HANNI FAKHOURY & ATTY. JENNIFER LYNCH, TEL. NO. 1-415-436-9333 APPEARING FOR THE PETITIONER;
ATTY. JONATHAN M. EISENBERG, DEPUTY ATTY GENERAL, TEL. NO. 1-213-897-6505, APPEARING FOR THE RESPONDENT.
RULING - ARGUED; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. RESPONDENT OFFICE OF THE ATTORNEY GENERAL'S Motion To Stay Case Pending Resolution Of Similar Litigation In Federal Court- Motion to stay granted in part and denied in part. Respondent's motion to stay the present matter pending the resolution of Electronic Frontier Foundation v. U.S. Department of Justice, U.S. Dist. Ct., No. Dist. Of Cal. Case No. 3: 15-cv-03186, is granted. A weighing of the factors set out in Caiafa Prof. Law Corp. v. State Farm Fire & Cas. Co. (1993) 15 Cal.App.4th 800, 804, indicates that a stay is appropriate. Although the parties in the present case and in Electronic Frontier Foundation v. U.S. Department of Justice are not identical, Electronic Frontier Foundation is the moving party in both cases. Furthermore, there is significant overlap between the document requests which are at issue in both cases. Both cases involve requests to law enforcement agencies, regarding Hemisphere Program training materials, guidance and policy documents, intergovernmental and related communications about the program, and contracts related to the program. Even though Electronic Frontier Foundation v. U.S. Department of Justice will be decided under Freedom of Information Act (FOIA), the decision will provide necessary guidance for the state court because the federal court's decisions will deal with the same types of documents and document requests about the same federal law enforcement program. (See BRV, Inc. v. Superior Court (2006) 143 Cal.App.4th 742, 756 (Federal cases interpreting Freedom of Information Act are helpful for state courts in construing the Public Records Act) ; County of Los Angeles v. Superior Court (2000) 82 Cal.App.4th 819, 825 (The Public Records Act and the Freedom of Information Act have similar policy objectives and should receive a parallel construction).) A stay is also warranted because although FOIA is not controlling in the state case, it is possible that the federal court could rule that a certain kind of record is properly withheld, while the present court could order disclosure of the same record. This court's order could therefore, potentially contradict and undermine the federal court's ruling. The fact that both cases are pending in the same location lends further support for staying the present case pending the resolution of Electronic Frontier Foundation v. U.S. Department of Justice. (Caiafa, supra, 15 Cal.App.4th at 804, 807.)
Respondent's motion to stay the present matter pending the resolution of Electronic Privacy Information Center v. Drug Enforcement Administration, U.S. Dist. Ct., Dist. Of Columbia, Case No. 14-cv-317, is denied. A weighing of the factors set out in Caiafa Prof. Law Corp. v. State Farm Fire & Cas. Co. (1993) 15 Cal.App.4th 800, 804, does not support granting a stay. None of the parties involved in the present case are litigants in Electronic Privacy Information Center v. Drug Enforcement Administration. The similarities between the document requests at issue in each case are not as pronounced as the similarities are between the requests in the present case and the requests in Electronic Frontier Foundation v. U.S. Department of Justice. Although Electronic Privacy Information Center v. Drug Enforcement Administration is in its second year of litigation, unlike Electronic Frontier Foundation v. U.S. Department of Justice, it is not pending in the same location as the present matter. (Caiafa, supra, 15 Cal.App.4th at 804, 807.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489
